stories.title: Import and export themes as JSON
acceptance_criteria: Settings > Theme shows a Download and Upload icon button next to the theme selector, both with aria-label and title tooltips

Clicking Download opens a native Save dialog defaulting to <slug>.todoozy-theme.json; cancelling is silent; success writes a pretty-printed JSON file matching the documented schema with both dark and light configs
Every built-in AND custom theme can be exported; exported file always contains both configs.dark and configs.light

Clicking Upload opens a native Open dialog filtered to .json; selecting a valid file creates two theme rows (one per mode) with new UUIDs, is_builtin: 0, and selects the mode-appropriate copy in the dropdown which applies it app-wide (same behavior as manual dropdown selection)
On successful import, a disappearing toast shows 'Theme loaded successfully'
On any validation failure, an error modal shows with the specific message and no theme rows are inserted (atomic)

Name collisions (against built-in OR custom themes, any mode) auto-append ' (Imported)', then ' (Imported 2)', ' (Imported 3)', etc.; both imported themes receive the same base suffix so they remain a matched pair
Escape closes the error modal; clicking 'Close' also closes it
Imported themes appear in the dropdown immediately and persist across app restarts
Imported themes are pushed to Supabase user_themes the same way a theme created via 'New Theme...' is
Vitest tests cover: slug generation (happy + edge), JSON validator (each failure class + success), name-collision resolver (no collision, single, multiple)
npm run typecheck passes with zero errors
Feature is fully usable via mouse; no keyboard shortcuts required

stories.title: Theme save icon only appears when color values actually differ from saved theme
stories.description:
In Settings → Theme, the Save (disk) icon next to the theme dropdown currently appears whenever the user switches themes in the dropdown, even if none of the color values have been edited. It should only appear when the edited color config differs from the saved config of the selected theme.

Expected behaviour:
- Picking a different theme from the dropdown (with no color edits) applies that theme across the app and does NOT show the save icon (the selection is persisted automatically, matching what the user already perceives — dropdown change = apply).
- Editing any color swatch / hex field → save icon appears.
- Clicking save persists color edits to the selected theme → save icon disappears.
- Reverting edits back to the saved values → save icon disappears (because current config equals saved config).

---

## Root cause

**File:** `src/renderer/src/features/settings/ThemeSettingsContent.tsx`

Lines 177–190 — `handlePresetChange`:

```typescript
const handlePresetChange = useCallback((themeId: string) => {
  const themes = useSettingsStore.getState().themes
  const theme = themes[themeId]
  if (theme) {
    setSelectedThemeId(themeId)
    setEditConfig(parseConfig(theme))
    applyThemeConfig(parseConfig(theme))
    // Mark as edited if switching to a different theme than the current one
    const currentId = useSettingsStore.getState().currentThemeId
    setConfigEdited(themeId !== currentId)
    setColorsEdited(false)
    setChangedKeys(new Set())
  }
}, [])
```

Line 186 sets `configEdited = true` on any dropdown switch, conflating two concepts:
1. "Theme selection changed" — should auto-persist, not require a save click
2. "Color values were edited" — is the real save-icon trigger

The render on line 503 keys off `configEdited`:

```tsx
{configEdited ? (
  <button onClick={handleApply} ... title="Save changes">
    <Save size={14} />
  </button>
) : canRevertSelected ? ( ... ) : canDeleteSelected ? ( ... ) : null}
```

That's why the icon appears after a simple dropdown switch with no color edits.

---

## The fix

### Step 1. Auto-persist the dropdown selection so switching applies globally

Replace `handlePresetChange` (lines 177–190) with:

```typescript
const handlePresetChange = useCallback(async (themeId: string) => {
  const themes = useSettingsStore.getState().themes
  const theme = themes[themeId]
  if (!theme) return
  setSelectedThemeId(themeId)
  setEditConfig(parseConfig(theme))
  applyThemeConfig(parseConfig(theme))
  // Auto-persist the selection — matches user expectation that
  // picking a theme from the dropdown applies it immediately.
  await setSetting('theme_id', themeId)
  await setSetting('theme_mode', theme.mode === 'light' ? 'light' : 'dark')
  setCurrentTheme(themeId)
  // No color edits yet — the save icon should NOT show.
  setConfigEdited(false)
  setColorsEdited(false)
  setChangedKeys(new Set())
}, [setSetting, setCurrentTheme])
```

The key changes versus the current code:
- Calls `setSetting('theme_id', ...)`, `setSetting('theme_mode', ...)`, and `setCurrentTheme(themeId)` so the selection is actually persisted on dropdown change (previously, switching only applied CSS and required the user to press save)
- Sets `setConfigEdited(false)` unconditionally — a dropdown switch is not a color edit

### Step 2. Make sure the mode toggle does the same (for consistency)

In `handleModeChange` (lines 156–175), when the counterpart theme is found and loaded, also auto-persist it so toggling mode applies the matched theme. Replace the `if (target) { ... }` block inside `handleModeChange` with:

```typescript
if (target) {
  setSelectedThemeId(target.id)
  setEditConfig(parseConfig(target))
  applyThemeConfig(parseConfig(target))
  await setSetting('theme_id', target.id)
  await setSetting('theme_mode', newMode)
  setCurrentTheme(target.id)
  setConfigEdited(false)
  setColorsEdited(false)
  setChangedKeys(new Set())
}
```

And change the function signature to `async` and extend the deps: `[currentTheme, setSetting, setCurrentTheme]`.

### Step 3. Keep the icon rule driven by real color-edit state

The current render at line 503 uses `configEdited`. With Step 1 in place, `configEdited` is only ever `true` as a result of `handleColorChange` (which already sets it at line 200). That is correct behavior. Leave the render condition as-is — no render change is required.

If you prefer defense-in-depth, you MAY additionally change line 503 from `{configEdited ? (` to `{colorsEdited ? (` — both flags are now set together. Either is acceptable; the simpler change is to leave the render alone since Step 1 prevents the bogus `configEdited = true`.

### Step 4. Verify `handleApply` still works

`handleApply` (lines 205–259) already reads `colorsEdited` to decide whether to push `updateTheme` with a new config (line 208). After the fix, `handleApply` is only invoked for genuine color edits, so the `if (colorsEdited)` branch is the normal path and the `setSetting('theme_id', ...)` lines 211–213 become a no-op idempotent re-apply (the dropdown already persisted the selection). That is safe.

---

## What NOT to do

- Do NOT add a debounce or timer for icon visibility — it's purely derived state
- Do NOT remove the `Apply Theme` button at the bottom (line 568) — it's still the canonical color-edit commit action
- Do NOT change how `handleColorChange` (line 194) sets `configEdited` and `colorsEdited` — color edits still correctly flip those flags
- Do NOT touch the counterpart-update toast logic in `handleApply` — it is out of scope

---

## Tests

No repository methods or utility functions are added, so no Vitest test file is strictly required. But if you add a new function (e.g., a pure helper that determines `isDirty(editConfig, savedConfig)`), write a unit test covering: identical configs → false; one key differs → true; all keys differ → true.

---

Before marking passes: true: read ui-reference.md and debug-learnings.md. Write tests for any new repository methods or utility functions. Run npm run test — all existing and new tests must pass. Run npm run typecheck — zero errors.
acceptance_criteria: Opening Settings → Theme with no changes shows either the RotateCcw, Trash2, or no icon next to the dropdown — never the Save icon
Selecting a different theme from the dropdown (with zero color edits) applies it app-wide and does NOT show the Save icon
Selecting a different theme persists theme_id/theme_mode immediately so a page reload would hydrate the newly-selected theme
Toggling between Dark and Light mode auto-applies the counterpart theme and does NOT show the Save icon
Editing any color swatch or hex field makes the Save icon appear
Clicking Save persists the edit; Save icon disappears and the selected theme row in the DB has the new config
Reverting edits back to the saved values (e.g., manually retyping the original hex) makes the Save icon disappear
Apply Theme button at the bottom of the panel still works for committing color edits
npm run typecheck passes with zero errors

stories.title: Cmd+K command palette matches against task UUID
stories.description:
The Cmd+K command palette currently only searches task titles. Users cannot paste or type a task UUID (full or partial) to locate a specific task. Add UUID substring matching alongside title matching so pasting `609ea5c2` (or any partial UUID) finds the matching task.

---

## Root cause

**File:** `src/renderer/src/features/command-palette/useCommandPaletteSearch.ts`

The text-search block is at lines 204–211:

```typescript
const filtered = allTasks.filter((task) => {
  // Text search: all text terms must match title (substring)
  if (parsed.textTerms.length > 0) {
    const titleLower = task.title.toLowerCase()
    if (!parsed.textTerms.every((term) => titleLower.includes(term))) {
      return false
    }
  }
  ...
})
```

Every query term is only matched against `task.title`. `task.id` is never checked, so UUID searches always return zero results.

---

## The fix

Replace lines 205–211 with a match that also checks `task.id`:

```typescript
// Text search: all text terms must match title OR task ID (substring)
if (parsed.textTerms.length > 0) {
  const titleLower = task.title.toLowerCase()
  const idLower = task.id.toLowerCase()
  if (!parsed.textTerms.every((term) => titleLower.includes(term) || idLower.includes(term))) {
    return false
  }
}
```

`parseQuery` (earlier in the same file) already routes bare tokens without a prefix operator (`@`, `#`, `!`, `priority:`, `label:`, etc.) to `parsed.textTerms`, so a pasted UUID like `609ea5c2-02c3-407a-9c86-3719992a692b` or a short prefix like `609ea5c2` lands in `textTerms` without any parser changes.

### Edge cases to preserve

- Partial UUIDs match. Typing `609ea5c2` finds tasks whose ID starts with that substring
- Multi-term queries still require ALL terms to match. If the user types `609ea5c2 bug`, it must match both a title containing `bug` OR an ID containing `bug` (unlikely) AND a title OR ID containing `609ea5c2`. The `.every(...)` operator handles that correctly
- Existing behavior (title-only searches) is unchanged for regular queries like `buy milk` — the `||` just adds a second matching axis
- Case-insensitivity is preserved by lowercasing both sides

---

## Tests

**File:** `src/renderer/src/features/command-palette/useCommandPaletteSearch.test.ts` — check if this file already exists. If it does, ADD new test cases. If it does not, create it following the style of other renderer test files (e.g., `src/renderer/src/shared/hooks/useThemeApplicator.test.ts`).

New test cases to cover:

- Full UUID match: a task with id `609ea5c2-02c3-407a-9c86-3719992a692b` is returned when the query is the full UUID
- Partial UUID (first 8 chars) match: query `609ea5c2` returns the same task
- UUID middle-substring match: query `3719992a` returns the same task (UUIDs are searched as substrings)
- UUID query does not false-positive on unrelated tasks: a second task with id `aaaaaaaa-bbbb-cccc-dddd-eeeeeeeeeeee` is NOT returned for query `609ea5c2`
- Title search still works: a task titled "Buy milk" is returned for query `milk` (regression guard)
- Mixed query: query `609ea5c2 urgent` returns a task only if it matches the UUID AND has `urgent` in title or ID

Since `useCommandPaletteSearch` is a React hook that takes tasks/filters as inputs, tests should either:
1. Import the internal `filterTasks` helper if one is extracted, OR
2. Use `@testing-library/react`'s `renderHook` to drive the hook with input tasks and a query.

If extracting a pure helper makes testing easier, extract the filter body into a named function like:

```typescript
export function matchesTextTerms(task: Task, textTerms: string[]): boolean {
  if (textTerms.length === 0) return true
  const titleLower = task.title.toLowerCase()
  const idLower = task.id.toLowerCase()
  return textTerms.every((term) => titleLower.includes(term) || idLower.includes(term))
}
```

and call it from the hook. Then tests only import and call `matchesTextTerms` — simpler and faster.

---

## What NOT to do

- Do NOT search `task.description` — out of scope for this story (different feature, different perf profile)
- Do NOT add a separate "ID lookup" input or keyboard shortcut — extend the existing Cmd+K palette
- Do NOT change the sort order — UUID matches sort alongside title matches by the existing priority + updated_at rules at lines 273+
- Do NOT change `parseQuery` — it already routes bare tokens to `textTerms`
- Do NOT add UUID-format validation (don't require the query to LOOK like a UUID before matching IDs) — substring match is good enough and handles partials

---

Before marking passes: true: read ui-reference.md and debug-learnings.md. Write tests for any new repository methods or utility functions. Run npm run test — all existing and new tests must pass. Run npm run typecheck — zero errors.
acceptance_criteria: Opening Cmd+K and pasting a full task UUID returns that task as a result
Opening Cmd+K and typing the first 8 characters of a task UUID (e.g., '609ea5c2') returns the matching task
Opening Cmd+K and typing a UUID substring from the middle (e.g., '3719992a') returns the matching task
Typing a UUID fragment does not return tasks whose IDs do not contain that fragment (no false positives)
Existing title-search behavior is unchanged — 'milk' still finds 'Buy milk'
Multi-term queries still require ALL terms to match (AND semantics preserved)
Unit tests cover: full-UUID match, partial-UUID match, middle-substring match, no-false-positive, title regression, multi-term AND
npm run typecheck passes with zero errors
npm run test passes with zero failures
